evals.prompt: I need to plan a migration from REST to GraphQL for our API layer. The endpoints are in src/api/ and we have about 15 routes. We want to keep backward compatibility during the transition.
evals.expected_output: A plan directory under {plansDir}/ with an index file containing phases, a subtask manifest with dependency ordering, and individual subtask files. The plan should address backward compatibility, phased migration, and testing strategy.
assertions: A plan directory was created under the configured plansDir with YYYYMMDD-feature-name format
An index file exists with the naming pattern plan-[feature-name]-[yyyymmdd].md
The index contains a Subtask Manifest table with columns: ID, File, Subagent, Dependencies, Phase
At least one subtask file exists for each row in the manifest table
Subtask IDs are zero-padded two-digit numbers
No subtask depends on a higher-numbered subtask ID
A mermaid dependency graph is included in the index
The plan status is set to Draft or Ready for Review
Each subtask file includes a Deliverables Checklist section
Backward compatibility is addressed in the plan requirements or key decisions

evals.prompt: hey can you plan out adding dark mode support to our Next.js app? the components are in src/components/ and we use tailwind. nothing fancy, just a toggle and persistent preference
evals.expected_output: A structured plan with subtasks covering theme provider setup, toggle component, Tailwind dark mode config, persistence (localStorage or similar), and updating existing components. Should be a small plan with 3-5 subtasks.
assertions: A plan directory was created following the YYYYMMDD-feature-name convention
The plan has between 2 and 8 subtasks (scoped appropriately for the request)
Each subtask has an assigned subagent from the allowed set (generalPurpose, explore, shell)
Phase assignments are consistent with dependencies
The index file contains a Definition of Done checklist
Subtask files follow the naming pattern subtask-[NN]-[feature]-[name]-[yyyymmdd].md

evals.prompt: Plan a comprehensive test infrastructure overhaul. We currently have no tests at all. The project is a Python FastAPI backend in src/ with 20+ endpoints, database models in src/models/, and business logic in src/services/. We need unit tests, integration tests, and CI setup.
evals.expected_output: A multi-phase plan with clear dependency ordering: test framework setup before test writing, unit tests before integration tests, CI setup after test infrastructure. Should include explore subagents for codebase analysis and generalPurpose for implementation.
assertions: The plan has at least 3 phases reflecting logical sequencing
An explore subagent is assigned for codebase analysis tasks
Test infrastructure setup subtasks come before test-writing subtasks in dependency order
The plan addresses unit tests, integration tests, and CI as separate concerns
The dependency graph has no circular dependencies
The index file overview mentions the current state (no existing tests)
Key decisions are presented covering test framework choice
